calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Off Calendar

Petition For Settlement Of Account
Off Calendar Aug-11-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Rachel Pozzi. Court appointed counsel Caroline Hinshaw for Daniel Pommon. Gary Vanderweghe for (trustee) John Kehriotis.

Ms. Navia reported that before the hearing she, Ms. Pozzi and Mr. Vanderweghe met to discuss the issue of distribution of income, that they are asking for regular distributions every 6 months of income and monthly distributions of rent and that they will defer the request of John Kehriotis for fees and costs to the court. Ms. Hinshaw stated that her interest is to make life better for Mr. Pommon, that she is trying to connect the dots from the 706 to this case and that she was told they could not have access to the records. Ms. Navia stated that she confirmed with Ms. Suzuki and the accountant that the information was not available but that they were able to provide verbal information. Mr. Vanderweghe stated that in regards to the tax return, it was filed in a separate estate, that it includes personal assets and that he has no authority over that estate to give out that information. Ms. Navia stated that they have nothing to add regarding the 706 or the tax issue and that they would like an annual accounting of the Danny Trust. Mr. Vanderweghe stated that the question of an accounting regarding the Dan Pommon Marital Trust is a given, that the trust is about to end, that they are just waiting for clearance on the tax return and the assets will be distributed. The Court asked if the parties are asking for additional time. Mr. Vanderweghe stated that this proceeding should end now, that the latest two items of continuing dialogue regarding contributions will be taken up with Ms. Pozzi and that it is entirely up to Mr. Kehriotis. Ms. Navia stated that they would like to be ensured of receipt of an annual accounting in the Danny Trust and Mr. Vanderweghe replied that it will be provided. The Court confirmed that nothing else needed to be set. The Court granted the petition to compel trustee to account and the petition for settlement of account (subject to review of the order).

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Continued To Jul-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for petitioner Rachel Pozzi; Gary Vandeweghe for trustee John Kehriotis; Caroline Hinshaw, court appointed attorney for Daniel Pommon.

Ms. Navia stated that she had filed objections to the accounting on Friday, June 10. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she had filed a joinder in the objections and requested a continuance. The Court ordered that any response by the Trustee to the objections must be filed by June 27, 2016, and any reply to that response must be filed by July 11, 2016. The matter was continued to July 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kevin Macksoud. (Not reported)

Petition For Settlement Of Account
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Continued To Jul-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Continued To Jun-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Settlement Of Account
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Continued To Jun-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Continued To Apr-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Rachel Pozzi. Gary Vandeweghe for (trustee) John Kehriotis. Court appointed counsel Caroline Hinshaw for and with Daniel Pommon, Jr.

The Court asked for Ms. Hinshaw's take on if an accounting should be provided. Ms. Hinshaw stated yes for reasons stated in the petition and to verify that what came out of the marital trust is correct. Ms. Navia stated that they have been asking for an accounting, that there is a long history, that there are two other trusts and that they do not want another issue with the court asking for information that they do not have. Mr. Vandeweghe stated that an accounting is no problem, that a report of information is a specific requirement and limited to beneficiaries, that Dan Pommon and the conservator are not beneficiaries, that if they just want information or an accounting they will provide it but that a report only goes to the beneficiaries. The Court asked about a petition to approve an accounting and Mr. Vandeweghe stated that he would be happy to do that and could file it within 3 weeks. Ms. Navia stated their acceptance if Mr. Kehriotis is willing to prove an accounting, that Daniel Pommon, Jr. is an income beneficiary of the trust, that they are allowed to request an accounting and that she would provide specific Probate Codes if necessary. The Court stated that a petition to approve an accounting is to be filed in 3 weeks, no later than March 16, 2016. Mr. Vandeweghe reported that they have now ended the Daniel Pommon, Sr. marital trust, that the accounting will be as of the date of death of Betty Pommon (1/4/15) until the assets are distributed, that distribution has been made with the exception of $1,100,000.00 which is being held as reserve for taxes. Ms. Navia confirmed that the accounting would be from Betty's date of death to the present date, that they would like ongoing accountings from the date assets were distributed to Mr. Kehriotis for the Daniel Pommon Trust. Mr. Vandeweghe stated that if they receive a request for a report of information they will comply and that they have told Ms. Navia this. Ms. Hinshaw stated that they would like an accounting of that trust. Mr. Vandeweghe stated that an accounting could be a lot of things and that they need an agreement on what is wanted. Ms. Navia stated that they want a court accounting. The Court stated that there is a consensus as to what is wanted, that it believes there is an agreement, requested that the accounting be a comprehensive as possible but that it will not order it. The petition to compel trustee to account; for removal of trustee was continued to April 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; For Removal Of Trustee
